controls.title: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
controls.description: Network security controls (NSCs), such as firewalls and network access control technologies, are used to restrict inbound and outbound network traffic based on business need.
evidence: Firewall configuration and rule set documentation
Network topology diagram showing segmentation
Firewall change management records
Quarterly firewall rule review records

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for installing and maintaining network security controls are defined and understood
Network security controls (NSCs) are configured and maintained
Network access to and from the cardholder data environment is restricted
Network connections between trusted and untrusted networks are controlled
Risks to the CDE from computing devices that are able to connect to both untrusted networks and the CDE are mitigated

controls.title: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components
controls.description: Malicious individuals often use vendor default passwords and other vendor default settings to compromise systems. These settings must be changed before installing a system on the network.
evidence: Hardening standard/baseline documentation per system type
Configuration compliance scan results
Inventory of all system components in scope
Procedures for managing vendor defaults

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for applying secure configurations are defined and understood
System components are configured and managed securely
Wireless environments are configured and managed securely

controls.title: Protect Stored Account Data
controls.description: Protection methods such as encryption, truncation, masking, and hashing are critical components of cardholder data protection at rest.
evidence: Data flow diagram showing all stored account data
Encryption key management procedures
DLP policy configuration showing cardholder data rules
Data retention and disposal policy

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for protecting stored account data are defined and understood
Storage of account data is kept to a minimum
Sensitive authentication data (SAD) is not retained after authorization
Access to displays of full PAN and ability to copy PAN are restricted
Primary account number (PAN) is secured wherever it is stored
Cryptographic keys used to protect stored account data are secured
Where cryptography is used to protect stored account data, key management processes and procedures covering all aspects of the key lifecycle are defined and implemented

controls.title: Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission
controls.description: Sensitive information must be encrypted during transmission over open, public networks, because it is easy and common for a malicious individual to intercept and/or divert data while in transit.
evidence: TLS/encryption configuration on all cardholder data transmission paths
Inventory of all transmissions of cardholder data
Certificate management records
Scan results confirming no unencrypted transmission

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for protecting cardholder data with strong cryptography during transmission are defined and understood
PAN is protected with strong cryptography during transmission

controls.title: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software
controls.description: Malicious software (malware) is installed through phishing, exploits, removable media, or other methods. Anti-malware solutions must be deployed and actively maintained.
evidence: Anti-malware deployment report covering all in-scope systems
Anti-malware update and scan logs
EDR alert review records
Phishing/email filtering configuration

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for protecting all systems and networks from malicious software are defined and understood
Malicious software (malware) is prevented, or detected and addressed
Anti-malware mechanisms and processes are active, maintained, and monitored
Anti-phishing mechanisms protect users against phishing attacks

controls.title: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
controls.description: Vulnerabilities in software can be exploited. Security patches must be installed, and software must be developed using secure coding practices.
evidence: Patch management policy and recent patch reports
Vulnerability scan results for all in-scope systems
Critical patch installation records (within 1 month)
Secure SDLC policy and evidence of code review

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for developing and maintaining secure systems and software are defined and understood
Bespoke and custom software are developed securely
Security vulnerabilities are identified and addressed
Public-facing web applications are protected against attacks
Changes to all system components are managed securely

controls.title: Restrict Access to System Components and Cardholder Data by Business Need to Know
controls.description: Systems and processes must ensure that access to cardholder data is limited to only those individuals whose job requires such access.
evidence: Access control policy and procedures
Role definitions and access matrices
User access review records (at least every 6 months)
Evidence of least-privilege enforcement

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for restricting access to system components and cardholder data by business need to know are defined and understood
Access to system components and data is appropriately defined and assigned
Access to system components and data is managed via an access control system(s)

controls.title: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components
controls.description: Assigning a unique identification (ID) to each person with access ensures that actions can be traced to a known individual. Multi-factor authentication is required for all non-console administrative access and access into the CDE.
evidence: Unique user ID policy and evidence of implementation
MFA configuration for all admin and CDE access
Password policy documentation
Inactive account review records

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for identifying users and authenticating access to system components are defined and understood
User identification and related accounts for users and administrators are strictly managed throughout an account's lifecycle
Strong authentication for users and administrators is established and managed
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is implemented to secure access into the CDE
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) systems are configured to prevent misuse
Use of application and system accounts and associated authentication factors is strictly managed

controls.title: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data
controls.description: Any physical access to data or systems housing cardholder data provides the opportunity for individuals to access devices or data and could allow removal of systems, and could allow removal of hardcopy materials.
evidence: Physical access control policy for data centers / CDE
Badge access logs for restricted areas
Visitor management log
Media destruction/disposal records

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for restricting physical access to cardholder data are defined and understood
Physical access controls manage entry into facilities and systems containing cardholder data
Physical access for personnel and visitors is authorized and managed
Media with cardholder data is securely stored, accessed, distributed, and destroyed
Point-of-interaction (POI) devices are protected from tampering and unauthorized substitution

controls.title: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
controls.description: Logging mechanisms and the ability to track user activities are critical in preventing, detecting, or minimizing the impact of a data compromise.
evidence: Audit log configuration for all in-scope systems
Log retention policy (minimum 12 months, 3 months immediately available)
SIEM alert rules and review records
Daily log review procedures and evidence

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for logging and monitoring all access to system components and cardholder data are defined and documented
Audit logs are implemented to support the detection of anomalies and suspicious activity, and the forensic analysis of events
Audit logs are protected from destruction and unauthorized modifications
Audit logs are reviewed to identify anomalies or suspicious activity
Retain audit log history for at least 12 months, with at least the most recent 3 months available for immediate analysis
Time-synchronization mechanisms support consistent time settings across all systems
Failures of critical security controls are detected, reported, and responded to promptly

controls.title: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
controls.description: Vulnerabilities are continually being discovered by malicious individuals and researchers. System components, processes, and software must be tested frequently to ensure controls are effective.
evidence: Internal and external vulnerability scan reports (quarterly)
Penetration test report (annual)
IDS/IPS detection rule review
Change-triggered scan evidence

sub_controls.title: Processes and mechanisms for regularly testing security of systems and networks are defined and understood
Wireless access points are managed
External and internal vulnerabilities are regularly identified, prioritized, and addressed
External and internal penetration testing is regularly performed, and exploitable vulnerabilities and security weaknesses are corrected
Network intrusions and unexpected file changes are detected and responded to
Unauthorized changes on payment pages are detected and responded to

controls.title: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs
controls.description: The organization must establish, publish, maintain, and disseminate a security policy and program addressing information security for all personnel.
evidence: Information security policy (reviewed annually)
Annual security awareness training completion records
Incident response plan and test records
Risk assessment documentation

sub_controls.title: A comprehensive information security policy that governs and provides direction for protection of the entity's information assets is known and current
Acceptable use policies for end-user technologies are defined and implemented
Risks to the cardholder data environment are formally identified, evaluated, and managed
PCI DSS compliance is managed
PCI DSS scope is documented and validated
Security awareness education is an ongoing activity
Personnel are screened to reduce risks from insider threats
Risk to information assets associated with third-party service provider (TPSP) relationships is managed
Third-party service providers (TPSPs) support their customers' PCI DSS compliance
Suspected and confirmed security incidents that could impact the CDE are responded to immediately
